revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]], in [[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Shinto shrine}}{{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]], in [[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Shinto shrine}}{{Authority control}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]]{{Hachiman Faith}}

三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]], in [[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]], in [[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. 三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]], in [[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]], in [[Sakyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyake Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Miyake Hachimangū]] [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]]

Recently, many pieces of large size of [[Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Miyake Hachimangū]] [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} [[ja:三宅八幡宮]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354999]] Wikidata name: Miyake Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], in [[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[:en:baby colic|baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[:en:baby colic|baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] }} [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} [[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11354999}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354999}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354999]] Wikidata name: Miyake Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], in [[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[:en:baby colic|baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[:en:baby colic|baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] }} [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} [[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354999}}

In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]] {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} [[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11354999}} [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354999]] Wikidata name: Miyake Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], in [[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as [[:en:baby colic|baby colic]], academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause [[:en:baby colic|baby colic]] (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema]] was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] built this shrine after his travel to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui court]] in the era of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]] {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} [[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11354999}} [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:qq]]

[[co:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}}|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, in [[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as {{ill|baby colic|en|baby colic|WD=Q1370346|lt=baby colic}}, academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause {{ill|baby colic|en|baby colic|WD=Q1370346|lt=baby colic}} (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema}} was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko}} built this shrine after his travel to the {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui court}} in the era of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]] {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} [[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11354999}} [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[co:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Miyake Hachiman-gu]][[en:Miyake Hachimangū]][[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|name=Miyake Hachimangū Shrine
三宅八幡宮|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Miyake Hachiman-gu haiden.jpg|270px]]|caption=The Miyake Hachimangū Shrine|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coord|35|4|6.08|N|135|47|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}|deity=[[Hachiman]]|festival=Autumn Festival, Hojou-e (放生会) (September 14,15, 16th)|type=[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]|leadership=|website=http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/|architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}}|founded_by=|established=859|date_destroyed=}} '''Miyake-Hachimangū''' (三宅八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, in [[:en:Sakyo-ku|Sakyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is famous for worship to beneficial to children, such as {{ill|baby colic|en|baby colic|WD=Q1370346|lt=baby colic|1=Baby colic}}, academic achievement, safe delivery of childbirth. Because Mushi (虫, parasitic worms, insects, bugs) was thought to cause {{ill|baby colic|en|baby colic|WD=Q1370346|lt=baby colic|1=Baby colic}} (疳の虫; kan-no mushi), the shrine also has worship for power to expel Mushi. Therefore the shrine is so-called Mushi-hachiman (虫八幡). Recently, many pieces of large size of {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema|1=Ema (Shinto)}} was excavated, that represent worship to expel Mushi, and the Ema were designated as National Folk Cultural Properties. == History == The shrine records say that {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko|1=Ono no Imoko}} built this shrine after his travel to the {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui court|1=Sui dynasty}} in the era of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}. In the journey to China, he got sick in the Chikushi region, and prayed for the recovery of his health at Usa Hachimangū. He then successfully accomplished his mission as ''kenzuishi''. He built Miyake Hachimangū in his territory to thank Hachiman. == Treasures == {{Empty section|date=June 2019}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} == Notes == == References == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20080916040843/http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/yurai.html Miyake hachimangu official web page, history] == External links == {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.miyake-hachiman.com/ Miyake Hachimangu] {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Miyake Hachimangū]] {{Commons category|Miyake Hachiman-gu}} [[ja:三宅八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11354999}} [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
